etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "speech") + phonetic 永(永) (yǒng) -> chant with speech
etymology.interpretation: 詠(詠) (yǒng, "chant; eulogize") combines the semantic component 言(言) (yán, "speech") with the phonetic 永(永) (yǒng). The character invokes the idea of chanting or composing with words (言) and uses 永 for its sound, suggesting an extended or enduring verbal expression, fitting for chanting or eulogizing.
etymology.simplification: 詠(詠) (yǒng, "chant; eulogize") was simplified to 咏(咏) by replacing the speech component 言(言) (yán, "speech") with the simpler 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") while retaining the phonetic 永(永) (yǒng).

examples.chinese: 他喜欢咏诗(他喜歡詠詩)。
我们常在聚会上咏颂古诗词(我們常在聚會上詠頌古詩詞)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen cháng zài jùhuì shàng yǒng sòng gǔ shī cí.
examples.english: We often chant and recite ancient poetry at gatherings.

examples.chinese: 咏叹调是歌剧的重要组成部分(詠嘆調是歌劇的重要組成部分)。
examples.pinyin: Yǒngtàndiào shì gējù de zhòngyào zǔchéng bùfèn.
examples.english: The aria is an important part of the opera.
